_comment: Baseline state for audits that grandfather pre-existing drift. New drift (post-baseline) fails CI; existing drift is tolerated until a deliberate sweep clears it. Update entries only when the corresponding drift has been resolved.

audit-changelog-currency.baselineNote: PR1 (cursor/nav-header-changelog-fix-d3c2) merge commit. Sets the floor for the changelog-currency audit; commits before this SHA may have un-recorded component file changes that we deliberately did not backfill.

audit-factory-version-currency._comment: Tracks whether commits that touch a FACTORY file (the five files whose edits can change factory OUTPUT, per uds-factory-versioning.mdc) also bump .cursor/figma/state/factory-version.json in the same commit. Pre-baseline history is grandfathered. New factory-file commits after the baseline must bump the version OR carry '[no-factory-bump: <reason>]' in the message. Re-baseline only on a deliberate decision.
audit-factory-version-currency.baselineNote: Moved from a96d0bd (2026.06.08.1 bump) to 07dc149. Grandfathers two output-neutral factory-file commits that corrected the field-metrics docs without bumping (and without the [no-factory-bump:] tag, which should have been used): 38364325 (design-language field-metrics 16/12 -> default-scale 14/10) and 07dc149 (resolve-token-value rule + design-at-default principle + factory-quality/skill pointers). These changed documentation/guidance only — the factory binds tokens, so a field always emitted font-size-base (14px at default scale); the '16px' was a wrong annotation of the larger-scale resolution, never a built value. The 2026.06.09.5 bar entry's numbers were corrected in the same sweep. Going forward, factory-file edits bump in the same commit or use the [no-factory-bump:] escape.

audit-css-api-table._comment: Components whose Code-tab API data in uds-docs/data/component-api/<id>.ts disagrees with retained <id>.css udc-* selectors as of 2026-05-12. The audit fails ONLY for components NOT in this tolerated list; new drift still fails. To remove an entry from this list, fix the API data or the retained CSS payload to match.
audit-css-api-table.baselineNote: Initial run of audit-css-api-table.sh against the post-PR1 state found pre-existing API-table drift across 10 components (some missing entries, some cross-component class references that are legitimate, some BEM sub-elements deliberately omitted). Backfilling all 10 is a separate sweep; for now they're tolerated and new drift fails. nav-header stays tolerated even after the 2026-05-13 bento removal because the audit's belongs_to family-fallback logic for nav-* classes is imperfect (it doesn't recognize sibling subparts like .udc-nav-account / .udc-nav-logo / .udc-nav-search as belonging to nav-header). The rigorous coherence check for nav-header is audit-doc-internal-consistency.sh, which DOES work correctly. A follow-up to fix the belongs_to family-fallback in audit-css-api-table.sh (one-line change) is filed separately.

audit-agent-docs-currency._comment: Tracks whether every .cursor/rules/*.mdc, .cursor/skills/*/SKILL.md, .cursor/agents/*.md is mentioned on uds-docs/app/(site)/cursor-workflows/CursorWorkflowsClient.tsx (and vice-versa). Pre-baseline commits are not enforced. Add file basenames to toleratedFiles for rules/skills/agents that intentionally do NOT appear on the docs page (e.g. internal-only or experimental). Update baseline only on a deliberate sweep that brings every file into the docs.
audit-agent-docs-currency.baselineNote: Set to PR1 merge commit. Initial pass after PR2 refreshed cursor-workflows.html to mirror all 14 rules, 8 skills, 7 subagents, 9 audits.

audit-doc-internal-consistency._comment: Per-component doc-side coherence audit (the safety net for partial cleanups after Figma deletions). Tolerated findings are pre-existing drift at the audit's introduction; new drift fails CI. Two tolerance modes: `toleratedComponents` (entire component skipped — use sparingly) and `toleratedFindings` (specific `comp/check/identifier` strings OR `comp/check/` prefix for an entire check on one component). The nav-header entries below will be resolved in the upcoming nav-header re-sync (bento removal); when that lands they MUST come off this list so the audit fully enforces.
audit-doc-internal-consistency.baselineNote: Audit introduced as Phase A of the figma-sync-removal-detection workflow upgrade. 14 pre-existing findings tolerated. Most are spec.json/impl.json drift in components under `uds-docs/uds/` (the source-of-truth-locked directory). Resolutions require Figma round-trips via sync-figma-component-spec, not autonomous edits.
